title: lazarum taylorii
side_effects: Lazarum taylorii can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing nausea, dizziness, and vomiting in some individuals, particularly when ingested in large quantities or without proper medical supervision. Additionally, excessive exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can lead to allergic reactions, including skin rashes, itching, and respiratory issues in sensitive individuals. Prolonged use or misuse of the Lazarum taylorii plant has also been linked to increased heart rate, tremors, and anxiety in susceptible people. In severe cases, improper use of the plant can even lead to life-threatening complications, such as seizures, coma, or even death.
